title: Did you think of the original while viewing
display_title: Did you think of the original while viewing
wikitext:
[]: Did you think of the original while viewing? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Nov 01, 2009 11:19 am Asking out of curiosity: Were you thinking of the original series while you were watching most of 2.0 or just Rebuild in general? For me: To be honest I wasn't thinking of NGE while I was watching most of 1.0 and 2.0. (Which can be considered odd I guess since 1.0 is pretty much 1 through 6 mostly )

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Nov 01, 2009 11:23 am I think it's pretty much inevitable for people who've seen the original. I wasn't mentally doing a scene-by-scene comparison or anything, but it's impossible to just erase the plot and characters of the TV series from your mind when watching Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Nov 01, 2009 11:31 am I couldn't help but compare 1.0 to NGE, they are extremely similar after all. To be honest though I was pretty blown away with 1.0 and thought that it improved on Ep5 and 6, Rebuild Ramiel was an epic finale compared to the monster-of-the-week feel the original had. 2.0, although I've only seen it once, was pretty different so I only really thought about NGE during the Bardiel and Zeruel scenes. I preferred Rebuild Bardiel to the series, without the whole "OMG DON'T TELL Shinji IT'S TOJI" stupidity (and making it Asuka this time makes sense), but I like both Zeruel battles on their own merits, the more impressive looking Rebuild scene, and the sheer brutality of NGE's fight.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Nov 01, 2009 1:17 pm I don't think your being truthful otherwise you'd probably be so confused with the plot right now that you wouldn't care. "Why the fuck are these monsters attacking!"

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 01, 2009 1:27 pm Lucretius wrote: I think it's pretty much inevitable for people who've seen the original. I wasn't mentally doing a scene-by-scene comparison or anything, but it's impossible to just erase the plot and characters of the TV series from your mind when watching Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Sun Nov 01, 2009 3:06 pm Axell wrote: "Why the fuck are these monsters attacking!"

[]:             Quote Post by icomeanon4 » Sun Nov 01, 2009 4:29 pm Sailor Star Dust"][quote="Lucretius wrote: I was at least mentally comparing what the 3.0 preview was indicating though: "Okay, so Shinji and Rei being trapped in Eva-01 is a new spin on what we see in Episode 20,

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Nov 01, 2009 4:34 pm icomeanon4 wrote: I can just picture this scene: Misato's fussing about how they have to get Shinji and Rei out of Unit 01, and the camera cuts to show Shinji and Rei lying naked on top of each other in the entry plug, and Shinji says, "No hurry, guys."

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Nov 01, 2009 5:54 pm Lucretius wrote: I wasn't mentally doing a scene-by-scene comparison or anything

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Sun Nov 01, 2009 5:57 pm I wasnt comparing but I do think that the series was adding additional character development for me. It was also cool to see how many series scenes they put in 2.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Nov 01, 2009 11:26 pm o yea my mistake. Still kinda proves my point though, I was assuming it hadnt been mentioned yet because in NGE we were still in the dark at that point. The line between them can sometimes blur

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Nov 02, 2009 2:01 am Kinda hard not to watch rebuild without thinking of NGE. In some instances rebuild plays off the fact that most of the audience has already seen NGE, such as foreshadowing of the battle with Unit-03 and the change of pilots. I mean there is little point to having Toji in the film at all other than to trick the audience into thinking he would be the pilot again.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 02, 2009 4:01 am As awesome as Rebuild Zeruel is it blows hard in comparison to original

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Nov 02, 2009 3:40 pm When I first saw Eva 1.0, I would say yes since I knew that it was a remake and I knew what will happen next. I have not seen the sequel yet though.

[]:             Quote Post by thefeelingofikarishinji » Mon Nov 02, 2009 4:43 pm Well, having seen the television series (and read through evageeks...) and knowing this was a re-take, I couldn't help but always have it in my mind, and be surprised at the changes and new things because I know the original so well. Though, I guess, with scenes like their visit to the aquarium, I did get caught up in just viewing the world of the new film. I agree that the many fakeouts--such as regarding Touji being a pilot, and eva 01 awakening as of the end of ep19 are surely built up with intentional suspense regarding what the audience thinks will happen next (I'd also put the Rei/Asuka elavator scene in there too.)

[]:             Quote Post by Cobra » Mon Nov 02, 2009 4:48 pm Watching most of You Are (Not) Alone, i will admit was a bit like playing spot the difference for a while. The it just became a case of ranting how incredibly awesome, and more powerful Ramiel is in the rebuild compared to the original. That was a key improvement for me really. Though some parts through me for a loop, like lilith being shown so early. meh, i need to watch the second one ASAP

[]:             Quote Post by Elmion » Fri Nov 13, 2009 5:20 am Yes, because I was watching it with a friend who had not watched the original NGE. It was quite a chore explaining why I made all those 'wtf' faces.

For me: To be honest I wasn't thinking of NGE while I was watching most of 1.0 and 2.0. (Which can be considered odd I guess since 1.0 is pretty much 1 through 6 mostly )
